sentence_reviews.text: Direct preference optimization and related objectives convert preferred–rejected response pairs into a policy update without an explicit online actor–critic loop.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO arXiv abstract lines 42-45 describes human relative-quality labels, fine-tuning LMs to align with preferences, solving the RLHF problem with a simple classification loss, and eliminating sampling from the LM during fine-tuning.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for DPO; 'related objectives' is broader but consistent with the DPO framing.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Direct and online preference optimization is modeled as depending on Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning and feeding downstream nodes such as Large language models, Conversational assistants & chatbots.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
